message:
fix(daemon): tune guard penalty scaling, suppress Green-pressure predictions, reduce log noise

Version bump 0.3.6 → 0.3.7

Policy engine guard-penalty recalibration based on production data from
vmi1156319, vmi1227854, and vmi1149989 (v0.3.5-6):
- Yellow pressure: guard penalty scale 1.0 → 0.25 — full penalty caused
  rejection deadlocks where the guard's permanent EWMA drift alarm
  vetoed every deletion candidate while disk usage kept climbing
- Orange pressure: guard penalty scale 0.25 → 0.10 — stronger candidates
  need to pass at this urgency level

Predictive warning suppression at Green pressure: EWMA rate spikes from
compilation bursts produced false "disk full in 5m" predictions when the
machine had >20% free space. These desensitize operators and flood logs.
The pressure controller will escalate to Yellow+ before real danger,
so Green-level predictions are now suppressed entirely.

Summary report enhancements:
- Added guard status (pass/FAIL/unknown/none) and policy mode to the
  periodic 5-minute summary log line for faster dashboard triage

Executor log throttling:
- Policy-reject log interval increased from 5 minutes to 30 minutes —
  on machines with permanent guard drift, the same rejection message
  repeated every scan cycle indefinitely, pure noise in the journal

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 [package]
 name = "storage_ballast_helper"
-version = "0.3.6"
+version = "0.3.7"
 edition = "2024"
 license = "MIT"
 description = "System service that prevents disk-full scenarios from coding agent swarms via ballast files, artifact scanning, and special location monitoring."
